pinDescription:
You're wearing just 20% of your closet 80% of the time—and that one stat changes everything about how you organize clothes.

Here's the truth most closet organization advice misses: the goal isn't owning less, it's seeing more. When your wardrobe organization works with you, those forgotten pieces in the back suddenly become your new favorite outfits. The simplest place to start? Group by type, then by color. It's one of those closet tips that feels almost too easy, but it instantly turns chaos into clarity and makes getting dressed feel effortless again.

When you declutter closet clutter and create a truly tidy wardrobe, mornings stop being a battle. You stop staring at a full closet thinking you have nothing to wear.

script: Teaching an AI to understand personal style turns out to be surprisingly hard. Not because the technology is complicated — but because style is deeply personal. What looks incredible on one person falls completely flat on another. The team behind Bons is solving exactly that: an app that learns your wardrobe, your body, and your lifestyle, then styles you as if it actually knows you. Because soon, it will. pinDescription:
Teaching an AI to truly understand your personal style is so much harder than it sounds — and that's exactly the challenge we fell in love with.

Here's a peek behind the scenes of our fashion startup journey. The hardest part of building Bons was never the technology. It was style itself, which is deeply personal. What looks incredible on one person can fall completely flat on another. So as a female founder navigating the messy, beautiful reality of app building, I became obsessed with one question: how do you teach an app to style someone as if it actually knows them?

That's the heart of Bons — an app that learns your wardrobe, your body, and your lifestyle, then dresses you like a friend who truly gets your taste. This is the part of the fashion entrepreneur path no one talks about, the slow work of building a brand that changes how people get dressed.

script: Okay, so I have one white button-down shirt and I'm about to show you three completely different outfits with it. First, tuck it into wide-leg trousers, add gold hoops, done — that's your dinner look. Now leave it open over a slip dress for that effortless layered thing everyone overthinks. And finally, knot it at the waist with denim shorts for the weekend. Same shirt, three vibes. This is exactly the kind of styling Bons does for your actual closet.
